scan_method: regex `\bsorry\b` minus comment-only mentions and string-literal occurrences

sorries.context: apparent_singularity_thm_i (proof of conjunct .1) -- supplies the `h_exact` argument to `frobenius_double_root_at_apparent_singularity` for s₁. Surrounding code: `exact frobenius_double_root_at_apparent_singularity a_coeff_c c_coeff_c s₁ root_s1 a_deriv_s1_ne_zero (by sorry)`
candidate_helpers: Thm66_ApparentSingularity.lean::hasDerivAt_a_coeff (real-variable HasDerivAt for a; complex analogue would parallel)
sorries.notes: Per fix_pass_log.md `h_exact sorry note`: the goal `∀ x, HasDerivAt (fun x => a x * deriv id x) (a x) x` is an artefact of the axiom formulation `frobenius_double_root_at_apparent_singularity` and cannot be discharged for `a = a_coeff_c` without `a(x) = C·e^x`. AEAL-status: blocked-by-design. Routing target is to REFORMULATE the axiom rather than discharge the sorry. Suggest splitting `frobenius_double_root_at_apparent_singularity` into a version that takes only `(ha_root, ha_simple)` and absorbs the derivative-shape hypothesis internally.

sorries.context: apparent_singularity_thm_i (proof of conjunct .2) -- supplies the `h_exact` argument to `frobenius_double_root_at_apparent_singularity` for s₂. Surrounding code: `exact frobenius_double_root_at_apparent_singularity a_coeff_c c_coeff_c s₂ root_s2 a_deriv_s2_ne_zero (by sorry)`
sorries.notes: Symmetric counterpart of S1 for the second indicial root. Same structural blocker (axiom-shape mismatch). If S1 is closed by axiom reformulation, S2 closes by the same edit. Treat S1 + S2 as a single discharge unit, not two independent ones.
